Elias Thorn stood at the bow of the Heron and watched the lighthouse of Port Saffron rise out of the morning fog. Elias Thorn had sailed into this harbor a hundred times, yet the sight of Port Saffron still tightened something in his chest. Behind him, Mira Vale folded her charts and joined him at the rail. "The Salt Guild doubled the harbor fee again," Mira Vale said. Elias Thorn shrugged. The Salt Guild owned every pier from here to the northern shoals, and arguing with the Salt Guild was like arguing with the tide.
